danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites with demonic appearance, grotesque silhouettes, red/brown discoloration
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to player; area is infested and hostile

danger_signals.visual_form: Hellish architecture, charred walls, demonic iconography, flesh-like textures
danger_signals.meaning: Demonic influence has corrupted the area; high danger zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Cyberdemon or Icon of Sin (massive sprite size, distinctive silhouette)
danger_signals.meaning: Apex threat; area contains major danger requiring confrontation

danger_signals.visual_form: Monster animations, attack patterns, directional facing toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat engagement required; threat is active

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing medkit sprites, health items on ground
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; player can sustain themselves in this area

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapon sprites placed throughout level, ammunition piles
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones or reward areas; player is encouraged to explore; tactical advantage available

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinctive glowing spheres, animated power-up sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Protected state or temporary invulnerability available; momentary safety window

safety_signals.visual_form: Military installations, base architecture, human-made materials
safety_signals.meaning: Last bastions of human control; areas not yet fully corrupted by Hell

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: UAC logo implied through base architecture
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military bases, technological installations, structured corridors, standard sci-fi architecture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Demonic iconography, pentagram-like symbols, hellish markings
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple demon types: Imps (small, red), Cyberdemons (mechanical-demonic hybrids), Barons of Hell (dark, armored), Hell Knights, Lost Souls (flying), Cacodemons (large eyes)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hellish dimensions, corrupted Earth locations, demonic structures, lava, flesh-like walls, dark stone

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Levels progress from human military bases to increasingly hellish environments; transition from ordered architecture to chaotic demonic landscape; environmental corruption gradient
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demons populate all levels; human resistance absent; player encounters only hostile entities; escalating demon complexity and numbers as Hell's influence deepens

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environment becomes progressively more hellish; later levels feature Hell dimension itself; visual complexity increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Monster types escalate; difficulty ramps; boss encounters placed at key narrative moments; interludes show story development between episodes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon and health pickups strategically placed; some areas heavily equipped, others sparse
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player rewarded for exploration off main path; nonlinear level design encourages discovery of hidden caches

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Areas with weapon/health pickups and no monsters = relative safety; demon-infested areas with scarce resources = danger
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player behavior shifts between exploration (safe) and combat (threat present)

environmental_storytelling.example: Levels shift from human military installations to unmistakable Hell dimensions, telling the story of invasion without dialogue
Weapon caches placed in specific locations suggest previous human defensive positions; their depletion suggests failed resistance
Encountering increasingly powerful demons across levels communicates Hell's growing dominance and the deteriorating human situation
Demonic symbols, hellish architecture, and corrupted textures layered over human spaces show demonic influence spreading
Story screens between levels provide context for the campaign narrative without breaking gameplay flow

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary or slow movement; facing random directions
Turn toward player; begin attack animation; launch projectiles
Slow but relentless movement; rocket launcher attacks; massive sprite presence dominates visual space
Melee and ranged attacks; coordinated movement; high aggression
Aerial movement patterns; erratic pathing; constant attack behavior once aware
Large floating presence; jaw-open attack posture; territorial behavior near spawning areas

notes: Doom II establishes its world-state through clear visual/environmental contrast between human-controlled infrastructure and demonic corruption. The game uses no explicit faction markers or allegiance systems; instead, player agency is implicit (you are human/military) and opposition is visually unambiguous (demons). Safety and danger are communicated through sprite presence, color palette shifts, and architectural style. The 32-level campaign structure (with secret levels accessible mid-game) allows environmental storytelling to show the progression of Hell's invasion from Earth locations to Hell itself. Monster types serve as both mechanical difficulty escalation and narrative indicators of increasing demonic presence.
